data.description:
## Why

`docs/architecture.md:49-55` states the core invariant: **all mutations go through
ONE locked append+commit path in `rebar._store`** (`event_append.py`, `push.py`,
`sync.py`, `lock.py`). `graph/_links.py` violates this — it hand-rolls a second,
divergent write path for LINK events. This is the single confirmed *structural*
finding of the 2026-06-18 audit (HIGH; the adversarial validator searched for a
justifying reason and found none). The risk is concrete: push policy, HLC
semantics, lock semantics, and commit shaping now live in two places, so any
change to `_store` silently fails to propagate to link writes, and the hand-rolled
copy already carries defects (wrong author, zero env_id, weaker lock).

## What

Route link-event writes in `graph/_links.py` through the canonical
`_store` write path (the same path `_commands/_seam.append_event` →
`_store.event_append.write_and_push` uses), eliminating the duplicate
lock+commit+push logic while preserving the pre-write cycle-check semantics.

## Scope

- In scope: `src/rebar/graph/_links.py` (`_write_link_event` / `add_dependency`),
  reusing `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`.
- Out of scope: changing link *semantics* (cycle rules, hierarchy promotion),
  the reconciler's grandfathered advisory lock, any non-link write.

## Context (citations)

- **The duplicate path:** `src/rebar/graph/_links.py:108-255` (`_write_link_event`).
  It builds the LINK envelope by hand, opens `.ticket-write.lock` with bare
  `fcntl.flock` (≈ lines 148-149), shells `git add` + `git commit --no-verify`,
  and runs its own retry/push/rebase/merge loop. It hardcodes
  `author="ticket-graph"` and `env_id="00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000"`.
- **The canonical path it should use:** `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:143`
  (`write_and_push`) → `:77` (`stage_and_commit`), which writes via atomic
  temp+rename, locks through `_store.lock.write_lock(tracker, dual_window=True)`
  (the unified **dual-leg fcntl+mkdir** lock, `_store/lock.py:1-29`), and runs the
  rebase guard (`check_no_rebase_in_progress`).
- **How other writers do it correctly:** `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py:151-187`
  (`append_event`) composes the envelope (real `author(...)`, real
  `env_id(tracker)`) and routes through the store path; `_commands/delete.py`
  writes UNLINK events this way.
- **Validator note:** the cycle checks (`_links.py:317-345`) run *before*
  `_write_link_event`, which already acquires its lock *internally* — so "check
  then write" is *already* non-atomic. A separate write path buys no atomicity;
  the differences are pure defects.

## Implementation plan

1. Read `_seam.append_event` (`_commands/_seam.py:151-187`) and
   `event_append.write_and_push` to confirm the envelope fields and return
   contract (exit code / event id).
2. **PREFER `_seam.append_event` over `write_and_push` directly.** The review
   confirmed `write_and_push(tracker, ticket_id, event)` takes a **pre-composed**
   event and does **NOT** tick HLC — so calling it directly would force `_links.py`
   to keep composing the envelope *and* ticking itself (the error-prone status quo).
   `_seam.append_event(source_id, "LINK", {"target_id":…, "relation":…}, tracker)`
   composes the envelope with real `author()`/`env_id(tracker)` **and** ticks HLC
   internally (`_seam.py:174`) — strictly cleaner. Replace `_write_link_event`'s
   body to call it, and **delete the existing `hlc.next_tick` call** (`_links.py:125`)
   so there is no double-tick.
3. Delete the now-dead inline `fcntl.flock` block, the `git add`/`commit`
   subprocess calls, and the bespoke push/rebase/merge retry loop.
4. Preserve the existing pre-call cycle-check / idempotency ordering in
   `add_dependency` exactly (do not move the check inside the lock — it is
   currently outside, and changing that is a separate design decision).
5. Verify the HLC tick source: `_links.py` currently calls
   `hlc.next_tick(...)`; confirm `write_and_push`/`stage_and_commit` performs the
   tick (or pass it through) so timestamps stay monotonic and no double-tick
   occurs.
6. Run the link/graph test suites (`tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py`,
   `tests/scripts/test_ticket_unblock.py`, any `tests/**/`*link* and
   `*graph*`), plus a two-clone convergence test if one exists, to confirm event
   shape and merge behavior are unchanged.

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** one write path; push/HLC/lock policy evolves in exactly one place;
  the author/env_id/rebase-guard defects vanish for free.
- **Cost/risk:** the extraction must preserve the "cycle-check (outside lock) →
  append (inside lock)" ordering and idempotency, so it is *not* a mechanical
  move — review the atomicity assumptions in `add_dependency` carefully. If
  `write_and_push` ticks HLC internally, ensure the old explicit `hlc.next_tick`
  call is removed to avoid a double tick.
- **Behavioral change to note:** LINK events will now carry the real `author` and
  `env_id` instead of the sentinel values. This applies to **both** writes —
  the primary link AND the `relates_to` reciprocal (both go through
  `_write_link_event`, e.g. `_links.py:369`). Confirm no test asserts on
  `author == "ticket-graph"` or the zero-UUID env_id; update any that do (they
  were asserting on a defect).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `graph/_links.py` no longer opens `.ticket-write.lock` directly, no longer
  shells `git add`/`commit`, and no longer implements its own push loop; it routes
  LINK writes through `_store.event_append.write_and_push` (or the `_seam`).
- [ ] LINK events carry a real `author` and a real `env_id(tracker)` (no
  `"ticket-graph"` / zero-UUID sentinels).
- [ ] HLC timestamps remain monotonic (no double-tick) — verified by a reducer/HLC test.
- [ ] All existing link/graph/unblock tests pass; two-clone link convergence (if a
  test exists) is unchanged.
- [ ] `ruff check src tests` and the scoped mypy gate stay green.
</content>
data.title: Route graph/_links.py link writes through the canonical _store write path
